nodes.title: Normalize errors to AbortError
nodes.why: This gives consistent abort errors whether they come from native abort or custom stale/cancel paths.
nodes.tradeoff: A helper adds a little code, but makes UI and tests much easier to handle.

nodes.title: Keep latest call state in closure
nodes.why: The wrapper needs shared state across calls to know which request is current.
nodes.alternative: Store state outside the function globally.
nodes.tradeoff: Closure state is isolated per wrapper instance, which is safer and reusable.

nodes.title: Version each call
nodes.why: Each invocation gets an id so settled results can be checked against the latest call.
nodes.tradeoff: Call ids add robustness when underlying work ignores abort signals.

nodes.title: Abort previous request before starting new one
nodes.why: This enforces takeLatest semantics: older in-flight work should stop when a newer call starts.
nodes.alternative: Let old requests continue and ignore their results later.
nodes.tradeoff: Aborting saves resources, but depends on worker code respecting `signal`.

nodes.title: Create and store a controller per call
nodes.why: Each call gets its own cancellation signal and becomes the new active request.
nodes.alternative: Reuse one controller for all calls.
nodes.tradeoff: Per-call controllers are correct for concurrent timing, with tiny object allocation overhead.

nodes.title: Pass AbortSignal into worker
nodes.why: The worker needs the signal to actually stop async work like fetch.
nodes.alternative: Do not expose signal to the worker.
nodes.tradeoff: Signal plumbing adds API shape requirements but enables real cancellation.

nodes.title: Reject stale settle paths
nodes.why: Even if older work resolves later, this check prevents stale values from updating state.
nodes.tradeoff: Extra guard code, but it closes race conditions when abort is not fully honored.
